name: SpectrumView Setup Guidance
description: Contextual guidance for setting up and using SpectrumView on Tektronix oscilloscopes. Includes command patterns, parameter relationships, and practical considerations for RF spectral analysis.
author: SpectrumView analysis script

handlerConfig.text: Use this guidance for SpectrumView setup, peak analysis, and power measurements on Tektronix oscilloscopes.

data.summary: SpectrumView enables FFT-based frequency domain analysis on oscilloscope channels, useful for RF signal characterization and spectral measurements.
sequenceHighlights: Enable SpectrumView on the desired channel before configuring frequency settings
Set center frequency and span to capture the frequency range of interest
Configure peak markers with appropriate threshold and excursion settings
Query peak results and convert dBm to voltage if needed
Use *OPC? commands to ensure settings are applied before proceeding
keyPoints: SpectrumView uses the oscilloscope's ADC to perform FFT analysis
Center frequency and span determine the frequency window of analysis
Peak markers identify the highest amplitude signals above threshold
dBm measurements use 50-ohm reference impedance by default
Voltage conversion requires understanding of power relationships

centerFrequency.description: Center of frequency analysis window in Hz
centerFrequency.typical: 1 MHz to 6 GHz depending on scope bandwidth

span.description: Width of frequency analysis window in Hz
span.guideline: Set wide enough to capture signal of interest

threshold.description: Minimum signal level for peak detection in dBm
threshold.guideline: Set above noise floor but below expected signal levels

excursion.description: Minimum peak height above surrounding noise in dB

pitfalls: Center frequency outside scope bandwidth produces no results
Span too narrow may miss signal, too wide reduces frequency resolution
Threshold too high misses valid peaks, too low picks up noise
Forgetting *OPC? can cause timing issues between commands
measurementConsiderations: Ensure proper channel termination (50 ohm for RF measurements)
Verify signal levels are within scope's dynamic range
Use appropriate record length for frequency resolution
Account for scope bandwidth limitations at high frequencies
recommendedApproach: Start with known signal to verify SpectrumView operation
Adjust threshold and excursion based on signal characteristics
Use wider span initially, then narrow for detailed analysis
Validate measurements with manual calculations for critical applications
verificationNeeded: Confirm SpectrumView support on specific scope model
Verify frequency range capabilities for your signals
Validate impedance settings match your measurement setup
Check if firmware updates affect SpectrumView behavior
relatedCommands: CH1:SV:WINDOW? - Window function used for FFT
ACQuire:SRATe? - Sample rate affects frequency range
